title: defparam or #() syntax do not modify the actual parameter
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

I recently used Yosys to generate netlist BLIFs in subcircuit format. I have passed the following files to Yosys and I expected the library circuit instance has the width I specified in the top module (top.v) in the output BLIF file. However, Yosys does not show such behaviour. It seems the defparam or #() syntax for redefining parameter do not work. Moreover, there are no `.names` statements that show the connectivity of single_port_ram output and the top_module output!
```
/* top.v */
module spram_instance(
	address,
	value_in,
	we,
	clock,
	value_out,
);

	parameter WIDTH = 16;	// Bit width
	parameter DEPTH = 8;	// Bit depth

/*  Input Declaration */
	input	[DEPTH-1:0] 	address;
	input 	[WIDTH-1:0]		value_in;
	input 	we;
	input   clock;

	/*  Output Declaration */
	output	[WIDTH-1:0] 	value_out;

	defparam inst1.ADDR_WIDTH = DEPTH; 
	defparam inst1.DATA_WIDTH = WIDTH;
	single_port_ram inst1 (
		.addr	( address ),
		.data	( value_in ),
		.we		( clock ),
		.clock  ( clock ),
		.out	( value_out )
	);

endmodule
```
-----
```
/* single_port_ram.v */
module single_port_ram #(
    parameter ADDR_WIDTH = 1,
    parameter DATA_WIDTH = 1
) (
    input [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] addr,
    input [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data,
    input we,
    input clock,
    output reg [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] out
);

    localparam MEM_DEPTH = 2 ** ADDR_WIDTH;

    reg [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] Mem[MEM_DEPTH-1:0];

    specify
        (clock*>out)="";
        $setup(addr, posedge clock, "");
        $setup(data, posedge clock, "");
        $setup(we, posedge clock, "");
        $hold(posedge clock, addr, "");
        $hold(posedge clock, data, "");
        $hold(posedge clock, we, "");
    endspecify
   
    always@(posedge clock) begin
        if(we) begin
            Mem[addr] = data;
        end
    	out = Mem[addr]; //New data read-during write behaviour (blocking assignments)
    end
   
endmodule
```
Commands to reproduce the issue:
```
        # load the library
	read_verilog -lib single_port_ram.v

	# read the top module
        read_verilog top.v; 
        hierarchy -check -auto-top

        # processing
	procs; opt; fsm; opt; check; memory; opt; 

        flatten; autoname;
	
        # write output
	write_blif -param -impltf output.blif;
```
## Expected behavior

*Please describe the behavior you would have expected from the tool.*
The _single_port_ram_ port sizes should be the same parameters defined in the top module. Moreover, wire _out_spram_instance_ exists.
```
/* expected BLIF file */
# Generated by Yosys 0.9+4008 (git sha1 1af99480, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

.model spram_instance
.inputs address[0] address[1] address[2] address[3] address[4] address[5] address[6] address[7] value_in[0] value_in[1] value_in[2] value_in[3] value_in[4] value_in[5] value_in[6] value_in[7] value_in[8] value_in[9] value_in[10] value_in[11] value_in[12] value_in[13] value_in[14] value_in[15] we clock
.outputs value_out[0] value_out[1] value_out[2] value_out[3] value_out[4] value_out[5] value_out[6] value_out[7] value_out[8] value_out[9] value_out[10] value_out[11] value_out[12] value_out[13] value_out[14] value_out[15]
.subckt single_port_ram addr[0]=address[0] addr[1]=address[1] addr[2]=address[2] addr[3]=address[3] addr[4]=address[4] addr[5]=address[5] addr[6]=address[6] addr[7]=address[7] clock=clock data[0]=value_in[0] data[1]=value_in[1] data[2]=value_in[2] data[3]=value_in[3] data[4]=value_in[4] data[5]=value_in[5] data[6]=value_in[6] data[7]=value_in[7] data[8]=value_in[8] data[9]=value_in[9] data[10]=value_in[10] data[11]=value_in[11] data[12]=value_in[12] data[13]=value_in[13] data[14]=value_in[14] data[15]=value_in[15] out[0]=out_spram_instance[0] out[1]=out_spram_instance[1] out[2]=out_spram_instance[2] out[3]=out_spram_instance[3] out[4]=out_spram_instance[4] out[5]=out_spram_instance[5] out[6]=out_spram_instance[6] out[7]=out_spram_instance[7] out[8]=out_spram_instance[8] out[9]=out_spram_instance[9] out[10]=out_spram_instance[10] out[11]=out_spram_instance[11] out[12]=out_spram_instance[12] out[13]=out_spram_instance[13] out[14]=out_spram_instance[14] out[15]=out_spram_instance[15] we=clock
.param ADDR_WIDTH 00000000000000000000000000001000
.param DATA_WIDTH 00000000000000000000000000010000
.names out_spram_instance[0] value_out[0]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[1] value_out[1]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[2] value_out[2]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[3] value_out[3]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[4] value_out[4]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[5] value_out[5]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[6] value_out[6]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[7] value_out[7]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[8] value_out[8]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[9] value_out[9]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[10] value_out[10]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[11] value_out[11]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[12] value_out[12]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[13] value_out[13]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[14] value_out[14]
1 1
.names out_spram_instance[15] value_out[15]
1 1
.end
```

## Actual behavior

*Please describe how the behavior you see differs from the expected behavior.*
The _single_port_ram_ port sizes are the ones defined as default in the single_port_ram.v
```
/* current behavior */
# Generated by Yosys 0.9+4008 (git sha1 1af99480, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

.model spram_instance
.inputs address[0] address[1] address[2] address[3] address[4] address[5] address[6] address[7] value_in[0] value_in[1] value_in[2] value_in[3] value_in[4] value_in[5] value_in[6] value_in[7] value_in[8] value_in[9] value_in[10] value_in[11] value_in[12] value_in[13] value_in[14] value_in[15] we clock
.outputs value_out[0] value_out[1] value_out[2] value_out[3] value_out[4] value_out[5] value_out[6] value_out[7] value_out[8] value_out[9] value_out[10] value_out[11] value_out[12] value_out[13] value_out[14] value_out[15]
.subckt single_port_ram addr[0]=address[0] clock=clock data[0]=value_in[0] out[0]=value_out[0] we=clock
.param ADDR_WIDTH 00000000000000000000000000001000
.param DATA_WIDTH 00000000000000000000000000010000
.end
```
